title: ipomoea nealleyi
intro: Ipomoea nealleyi, also known as the wild potato vine or purple passion vine, is a perennial vine native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions and wounds, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as acting as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, burns, and infections.
